description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a yellow fuselage and dual wings, featuring prominent U.S. military roundels on the upper and lower wings. The aircraft has a radial engine with an exposed propeller at the front, and its landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel. The tail section is painted blue with the number '88' in white, and a red band wraps around the rear fuselage. The wings are straight and stacked vertically, connected by struts and wires, giving the plane a classic early 20th-century appearance. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely accommodating a single pilot. The surface of the airplane shows weathering and panel lines, adding to its realistic, well-used look.
description: A yellow vintage biplane with dual wings, exposed radial engine, and U.S. military markings. Features an open cockpit and blue tail with the number '88'.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, exposed radial engine, and propeller, with landing gear visible below.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the open cockpit, blue tail, red fuselage band, and the number '88' on the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the blue tail, red band, and both upper and lower wings with roundels, as well as the tailwheel.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other side of the fuselage, cockpit, and landing gear, with the propeller and engine visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, staggered wings stacked vertically and supported by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted radial piston engine with exposed cylinders and a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; blue rudder and elevators.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small fixed tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage, likely single-seat with no canopy.
